title: Dryopteris uniformis
taxonomy: Dryopteris uniformis is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is a terrestrial fern that belongs to the genus Dryopteris, which includes over 200 species of ferns. The family Dryopteridaceae is a group of ferns commonly known as the wood ferns or shield ferns. Dryopteris uniformis is native to China and is a relatively rare species. The classification of Dryopteris uniformis is as a species within the family Dryopteridaceae and the genus Dryopteris.
uses: Dryopteris uniformis, also known as the Uniform Wood Fern, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The plant's leaves and stems are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Uniform Wood Fern for medicinal purposes.
